text:
I’ve heard bad things about ACC so far… Is off campus a better bet?
Ants, mildew, ripped furniture, just a couple of things I’ve heard from people who just moved in. Is a more expensive apartment worth it ?

ACC is better. I’m not living in luxury but it’s a roof over my head, and it’s cheap. So I’m not gonna complain. I only have to pay for electricity, and I get a stipend for laundry (puerto). No complaints so far.

I’m not living in luxury

ACC apartment’s are quiet nice actually. I'm a grad student living in grad housing rn, but I have lived in five other University adjacent apartments and have seen dozens of others via friends.

ACC is probably some of the nicest I have seen. We had ACC near my undergrad campus and they were a dump compared to UCI's ACC. We're always trying to hang out at the ACC pools and such. When I posted the ACC pools on my insta, my undergrad friends where like "WTF, that's on a university campus."

You pay for it, but they are nice.

Tbh, yeah I definitely agree! I meant like I’m not living in anything grand. But compared to most university apartments this is very nice! Compared to my own house they are pretty nice 🤷🏽‍♂️. Idk what standard of living people are used to, but ACC really isn’t bad whatsoever.

If you can get plaza verde then I would say it’s better than off campus given its price

Second this. I am quite comfy at Plaza Verde. Though I've only been here for a week and a half, Im hoping to keep the same room for next year. I feel like a lot of posts about ACC's do not necessarily apply to Plaza Verde. I could also be very lucky, though.

Just remember that the parking pass is around $350 a quarter. Electricity is also a separate bill.

From my own experience and my friends', ACC is a much better in terms of price and overall worth. Off-campus works, but unless you're planning on spending $1500-$2000 minimum for a private bedroom, you'll be sharing a room with someone. For ACC apartments, Plaza Verde comes in at 1st and Camino del Sol at 2nd, the rest are all up in the air in my opinion.

camino del all is very nice so far besides the couches the furniture is pretty solid, there’s ac, large spaces, nice bathrooms, decent amenities, etc the wifi sucks but it’s cheaper and more convenient than living off campus

Honestly no, with acc you have a reliable form of transportation which is the anteater express that passes through all the properties, it might be full sometimes but it’s better than walking 20-30 min from an off campus apartment. There’s also the 24 hr service, if you get locked out at 3 am someone can let you back in. Sure it has its downsides but a single room is also cheaper than off campus housing.

It’s a livable place, it isn’t luxurious but it has practically every commodity you can ask for. Also if your roommates decide to move out you won’t be affected, you only pay for your space. There’s a sense of security with acc that off campus doesn’t have.

PLUS it’s furnished! And for some properties you don’t pay any utilities

I think a lot of what you see on Reddit is people complaining when they have a problem. They’re a decent place to live, especially if you have good roommates. They’ll fix stuff promptly, the AC and appliances work, and being able to go down to the pool whenever you want to (VDCN) is nice.

Honestly, the roommates you get make or break your experience. Use roomSync and talk to people in advance.

THIS! You worded it amazingly!!

I would stick to ACC… I live off campus and it’s very expensive compared to ACC. You might have shit Wi-Fi at ACC from what I’ve witnessed thruout this Reddit pg but at least you have a place that’s already furnished and with the utilities already embedded or at little cost. I already had to spend money to furnish my place, the application fee, security deposit, trash fee, do laundry, and water, electricity, Wi-Fi, and gas are all separate utilities that need to be payed.

A single room with a shared bathroom at my place would be 1600$ if u have no roommate and this is not even the master bedroom. There’s not even any embedded light fixtures in the bedrooms so u got to buy ur own lamps. I believe Camino or PV are one of the nicer units. The only complaint I personally have from visiting ACC is the atrocious smell. I think ppl at ACC should be grateful that they got the opportunity to live there compared to utc or literally anywhere else in Irvine.
I bet you can find any room off campus at $1500 a month without utility right now with or without roommates.
